content.content:
# "Just Use Grid" Option Analysis

**Article Date:** October 21, 2024

## Core Syntax Addition

The "Just Use Grid" option does **not** require a new display type. Instead, it adds a single new value to existing properties:

> "The Just Use Grid option adds _only_ _one new value_ to CSS. You can create masonry-style layouts by simply using the `collapse` value in a track definition"

The new syntax uses existing grid properties with this additional value:

```css
grid-template-rows: collapse  /* or grid-template-columns: collapse */
```

## Example Comparison

The article provides this direct contrast. Using the "Just Use Grid" approach for a 3-column masonry layout:

> ```
> .masonry {
>   display: grid;
>   grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
>   grid-template-rows: masonry; /* final name TBD */
>   gap: 10px;
> }
> ```

## Key Design Philosophy

Rather than introducing redundant properties, the approach leverages existing CSS Grid infrastructure:

> "By not repurposing what's already there, New Masonry Layout creates unnecessary redundancy in CSS."

This maintains consistency with CSS design principles emphasizing minimal redundancy and reuse of established patterns.
